enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); RNEXT '=' is a spec-allowed synonym for 'same as RNAME'

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR ops preserves total query and reference consumption lengths

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR query-consuming length mismatch with SEQ length
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise validation error or crash) when the sum of query-consuming CIGAR operators (M, I, S, =, X) does not equal len(SEQ) and SEQ != '*'. Silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: If not a '*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.
Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

enforced.mr_name: POS shift with synchronized SQ LN preserves alignment validity
enforced.oracle: After shifting all POS values by +shift and increasing each @SQ LN by +shift, every alignment record's POS + sum_of_reference_consuming_CIGAR_ops (M, D, N, =, X) must be <= the new LN. Records with POS=0 or RNAME='*' are unchanged. The set of valid alignments (those not exceeding LN) is preserved.

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base (see table below). The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1. POS is set as 0 for an unmapped read without coordinate.
LN* & Reference sequence length. Range: $[1,\,2^31-1]$
``Consumes query'' and ``consumes reference'' indicate whether the CIGAR operation causes the alignment to step along the query sequence and the reference sequence respectively.

enforced.oracle: After round-tripping through BAM (sam->bam->sam), every alignment record's POS field must be identical to the original. The 1-based coordinate system must survive binary encoding/decoding without off-by-one errors. PNEXT must also be preserved identically.

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base (see table below). The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1.
PNEXT: 1-based Position of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. Set as 0 when the information is unavailable.

enforced.mr_name: RNEXT '=' alias round-trip invariance under BAM codec
enforced.oracle: P(parse(T(x))) == P(parse(x)) where P normalizes RNEXT '=' to explicit RNAME. The BAM codec must preserve the RNEXT field semantics regardless of whether '=' or explicit RNAME is used in the text representation.

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: Mate flag stripping for unpaired reads round-trip invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P clears mate-context bits (0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x80) when FLAG 0x1 (multiple segments) is unset. SAMv1 §1.4 p47: these bits are only meaningful when 0x1 is set.

evidence.quote: If 0x1 is unset, no assumptions can be made about 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40 and 0x80.

enforced.mr_name: SEQ case normalization preserves sequence query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method, where T uppercases SEQ field. The sequence content is semantically identical regardless of letter case per SAM spec.

evidence.quote: Each alignment line has 11 mandatory fields for essential alignment information such as mapping position, and variable number of optional fields for flexible or aligner specific information.

enforced.mr_name: Optional tag field permutation preserves alignment query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method, where T shuffles optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields. The 11 mandatory fields and alignment semantics are unchanged.

evidence.quote: Each alignment line has 11 mandatory fields for essential alignment information such as mapping position, and variable number of optional fields for flexible or aligner specific information.
As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

enforced.mr_name: RNEXT equals alias canonicalization preserves alignment query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method, where T toggles RNEXT between '=' and explicit RNAME. SAMv1 explicitly allows '=' as a synonym for 'same as RNAME'.

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field order invariance
quarantine.oracle: After permuting the order of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields on each alignment line, the parsed record's biological semantics are unchanged. All SUTs must produce identical query results for the same record regardless of optional field ordering.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.
Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within the @HD header line, the parsed header semantics are unchanged. All SUTs must produce identical header interpretation regardless of subtag ordering within @HD.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @SQ header line, the parsed reference sequence dictionary is unchanged. All SUTs must produce identical @SQ metadata regardless of subtag ordering within each @SQ line.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @RG header line, the parsed read-group metadata is unchanged. All SUTs must produce identical @RG information regardless of subtag ordering within each @RG line.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs within each @PG header line, the parsed program record metadata is unchanged. All SUTs must produce identical @PG information regardless of subtag ordering within each @PG line.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the order of @CO comment lines in the header, the parsed biological semantics are unchanged. @CO lines are free-text comments with no ordering semantics per the SAM spec.

evidence.quote: In the header, each line is TAB-delimited and, apart from @CO lines, each data field follows a format `TAG:VALUE' where TAG is a two-character string that defines the format and content of VALUE. Thus header lines match /^@(HD|SQ|RG|PG)(\t[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]:[ -~]+)+$/ or /^@CO\t.*/.

ambiguity_flags: @CO lines are explicitly excluded from the TAG:VALUE format, and the spec does not impose any ordering constraints on @CO lines, but there is no explicit statement that @CO order is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header record type order invariance (excluding @HD)
quarantine.oracle: After reordering @SQ and @RG header lines among themselves (while keeping @HD first), the parsed biological semantics are unchanged. The SAM spec only mandates @HD be first; other record types have no required ordering.

evidence.quote: @HD & File-level metadata. Optional. If present, there must be only one @HD line and it must be the first line of the file.

ambiguity_flags: The spec explicitly requires @HD to be first but does not specify ordering among @SQ, @RG, @PG lines. However, @SQ order defines the coordinate sort order (SO:coordinate), so reordering @SQ lines may affect coordinate-sorted alignment interpretation.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); SAM v1.5 and v1.6 are backwards-compatible for files not using 1.6-only features

evidence.quote: VN* & Format version. Accepted format: /94[0-9]+92.[0-9]+$/.

ambiguity_flags: SAM v1.5→1.6 added clarifications but kept backwards-compatibility; some SUTs may version-gate parser behavior on VN

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))); SAM spec permits both upper and lower case in SEQ field

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: SAM spec regex permits both cases but some tools may be case-sensitive

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P normalizes clipping representation. H<->S conversion preserves the underlying alignment semantics: H->S pads dummy bases in SEQ/QUAL, S->H trims them, and the core alignment (M/D/N/=X ops) remains unchanged.

evidence.quote: H can only be present as the first and/or last operation. S may only have H operations between them and the ends of the CIGAR string.

ambiguity_flags: H->S conversion requires adding dummy bases to SEQ/QUAL which changes the raw record text, but the alignment semantics (mapped bases) are preserved. Some parsers may treat H and S differently for certain calculations.

quarantine.mr_name: Flag bit 0x4 unmapped inconsistency with RNAME and POS
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise validation error or crash) when FLAG 0x4 is set on a record with RNAME != '*' and POS > 0, as this violates the spec rule that unmapped segments must have RNAME='*' and POS=0. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.
An unmapped segment without coordinate has a '*' at this field. However, an unmapped segment may also have an ordinary coordinate such that it can be placed at a desired position after sorting.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'no assumptions can be made' when 0x4 is set, which some parsers may interpret as allowing any RNAME/POS values rather than requiring rejection. However, the normative convention is that unmapped reads with coordinate have RNAME='*' and POS=0, and setting 0x4 on a mapped read is a contradiction.

quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise validation error or crash) when an optional tag's type character is not one of the valid types (A, i, f, Z, H, B). Silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

ambiguity_flags: The spec chunk retrieved does not explicitly enumerate the valid type characters (A,i,f,Z,H,B) — this is defined in the SAMtags section which was not retrieved below threshold. The rule is well-established in the SAM specification but the evidence here is indirect.

quarantine.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired reads
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise validation error or crash) when two reads of a paired template have TLEN values with the same sign (both positive or both negative), violating the rule that the two ends must have differing signs. Silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

ambiguity_flags: The rule applies only when both reads of a template are mapped to the same reference sequence. When TLEN is 0 (information unavailable), the rule does not apply.

quarantine.mr_name: CRAM round-trip preserves coordinate system under lossy normalization
quarantine.oracle: After round-tripping through CRAM (sam->cram->sam), the POS field of every alignment record must be preserved identically. CRAM may collapse =/X to M in CIGAR, but the reference-consuming length and thus the implied end coordinate (POS + ref_consuming_length - 1) must be invariant. The canonical normalizer's cram_safe mode accounts for =/X->M collapse.

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base (see table below). The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1.
``Consumes query'' and ``consumes reference'' indicate whether the CIGAR operation causes the alignment to step along the query sequence and the reference sequence respectively.

ambiguity_flags: CRAM may collapse =/X CIGAR ops to M, which changes CIGAR representation but preserves reference-consuming length

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P strips zero-length CIGAR ops and merges adjacent same-type ops. Zero-length CIGAR ops carry no alignment information and should be removed by canonical normalization.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: SAMv1 §1.4.6 does not explicitly forbid zero-length CIGAR ops, but the SAM Recommended Practice recommends adjacent CIGAR operations should be different

quarantine.mr_name: Unmapped record MAPQ normalization round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P sets MAPQ to 255 for unmapped reads (FLAG 0x4 set). SAMv1 §1.4 specifies MAPQ 255 means 'mapping quality is not available', which applies to unmapped reads.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

ambiguity_flags: MAPQ 255 is defined as 'not available' but the spec does not mandate it specifically for unmapped reads; it's a convention

quarantine.mr_name: Header @CO comment reordering preserves alignment query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method, where T shuffles @CO comment lines. SAMv1 declares @CO to be free-text comments with no ordering semantics.

evidence.quote: SAM stands for Sequence Alignment/Map format. It is a TAB-delimited text format consisting of a header section, which is optional, and an alignment section.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p129
evidence.quote: This section describes the best practice for representing data in the SAM format. They are not required in general, but may be required by a specific software package for it to function properly.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: @CO lines are described as 'unordered' in the spec table but the exact quote about ordering semantics is from the Recommended Practice section
